title: Unit Final: Goal Setting and Career Planning

questions.prompt: What is the main purpose of using SMART goals in planning?

choices.text: To make goals clearer, more organized, and easier to track
choices.feedback: Correct. SMART goals help students create goals that are clear, realistic, and trackable.

choices.text: To avoid setting deadlines
choices.feedback: SMART goals include deadlines because they are time-bound.

choices.text: To replace all action steps with general ideas
choices.feedback: SMART goals should support action steps, not replace them.

choices.text: To make plans less specific
choices.feedback: SMART goals are meant to be more specific, not less.

questions.prompt: A long-term goal usually needs short-term steps to support it.

choices.text: False
choices.feedback: Long-term goals are usually stronger when supported by short-term steps.

choices.text: True
choices.feedback: Correct. Short-term steps help students make progress toward a long-term goal.

questions.prompt: Which example best shows academic planning for a career goal?

choices.text: Ignoring course choices until the last year of school
choices.feedback: Academic planning involves making thoughtful school choices before the last minute.

choices.text: Taking classes and using strategies that support a future goal
choices.feedback: This idea is related, but it does not best explain how school planning connects to career goals.

choices.text: Choosing school options that support future career preparation
choices.feedback: Correct. Academic planning means choosing courses and preparation steps that connect to future goals.

choices.text: Selecting classes without thinking about future plans
choices.feedback: Academic planning should connect current choices to future goals.

questions.prompt: A student wants to become a nurse. The student sets a goal to improve science grades this semester, meet with a counselor next month, and review future course choices before the next school year. What does this best show?

choices.text: A plan with no connection to a larger goal
choices.feedback: The steps clearly connect to a larger career goal.

choices.text: A mix of goal setting, short-term planning, and academic preparation
choices.feedback: Correct. This scenario combines a future goal with short-term steps and academic planning.

choices.text: A barrier that cannot be solved
choices.feedback: The scenario shows planning, not an unsolved barrier.

choices.text: A plan that avoids timelines
choices.feedback: The scenario includes clear time references.

questions.prompt: A timeline can help students organize the order of steps in a plan.

choices.text: True
choices.feedback: Correct. Timelines help students organize when different steps should happen.

choices.text: False
choices.feedback: Timelines are useful because they help organize the sequence of steps.

choices.text: Avoiding all planning decisions
choices.feedback: Adaptability does not mean avoiding planning.

choices.text: Changing direction whenever a task feels difficult
choices.feedback: Adaptability involves thoughtful adjustment, not changing direction every time work is hard.

choices.text: The ability to adjust a plan when conditions or barriers change
choices.feedback: Correct. Adaptability means adjusting a plan when conditions change.

choices.text: Following one plan forever without changes
choices.feedback: Adaptability requires flexibility when needed.

questions.prompt: A student planned to take part in a career club, but now needs after-school tutoring at the same time. What is the best response?

choices.text: Identify the conflict as a barrier and adjust the plan to keep making progress
choices.feedback: This idea is related, but it does not best emphasize problem-solving and adjustment.

choices.text: Give up on the larger goal completely
choices.feedback: A schedule conflict does not automatically mean the larger goal should end.

choices.text: Ignore the conflict and hope the plan works anyway
choices.feedback: Ignoring a barrier is not a strong planning response.

choices.text: Use problem-solving to adjust the plan while keeping the larger goal in mind
choices.feedback: Correct. Strong planning includes identifying the barrier and adjusting the plan while staying focused on the larger goal.

questions.prompt: Why is it helpful to connect school choices to a future career goal?

choices.text: Because school choices never affect future preparation
choices.feedback: School choices can support future preparation.

choices.text: Because students should only think about the future after graduation
choices.feedback: Future planning can begin before graduation.

choices.text: Because school choices can help build preparation for later opportunities
choices.feedback: Correct. School choices can help students prepare for future opportunities and goals.

choices.text: Because course selection guarantees one final career outcome
choices.feedback: Course selection can support preparation, but it does not guarantee one final outcome.

questions.prompt: Adjusting a plan always means the original goal was a failure.

choices.text: True
choices.feedback: This is incorrect. Adjusting a plan can show strong problem-solving and adaptability.

choices.text: False
choices.feedback: Correct. Adjusting a plan can be a responsible response to barriers.

questions.prompt: A student writes a SMART goal, creates a timeline, chooses supportive classes, and identifies one possible barrier with an adjustment. What does this best show?

choices.text: A complete career planning process using several Unit 3 skills together
choices.feedback: This idea is related, but it does not best represent a structured plan using multiple unit concepts.

choices.text: A plan focused only on one short-term task
choices.feedback: The scenario includes more than one planning element.

choices.text: A structured plan that combines goals, steps, academic choices, and flexibility
choices.feedback: This idea is related, but it does not best show how multiple planning skills work together.

choices.text: An organized career plan that uses multiple planning tools together
choices.feedback: Correct. This scenario shows a structured career plan using multiple Unit 3 planning tools together.
